scientific_method.title: The Scientific Method - Observation and Discovery
scientific_method.scripture_connection: Proverbs 25:2 - It is the glory of God to conceal a matter; to search out a matter is the glory of kings.

experiment.objective: Learn and practice the scientific method through careful observation, hypothesis formation, experimentation, and conclusion - the tools God gave us to explore His creation.
materials: Natural objects to observe (leaves, rocks, insects, etc.)
procedure: OBSERVE: Go outside and carefully observe something in nature for 5 minutes. Write down everything you notice - colors, textures, sizes, behaviors, smells.
QUESTION: Based on your observations, write down a question you want to answer. Example: 'Why do leaves change color in fall?' or 'How do ants find food?'
HYPOTHESIZE: Make an educated guess (hypothesis) about the answer to your question. Write it as an 'If... then...' statement.
EXPERIMENT: Design a simple test to check your hypothesis. What will you measure? What will you keep the same? What will you change?
RECORD DATA: Carry out your experiment and write down exactly what happens. Use numbers and measurements when possible.
CONCLUDE: Did your results support your hypothesis? What did you learn? What new questions do you have?
experiment.observations: Students will discover that careful observation is the foundation of all science. They will learn that the scientific method is a systematic way to ask questions about God's creation and find reliable answers.
experiment.biblical_worldview: The scientific method works because God created an orderly, consistent universe that follows laws He established. Science is possible because creation reflects the mind of a rational Creator. When we do science, we are 'thinking God's thoughts after Him' as Johannes Kepler said. Observation, the first step of the scientific method, is an act of worship - paying attention to what God has made.

seed_germination.title: Seed Germination - God's Design in Plant Life
seed_germination.scripture_connection: Genesis 1:11-12 - Then God said, 'Let the earth sprout vegetation, plants yielding seed, and fruit trees bearing fruit after their kind with seed in them, on the earth'; and it was so.

experiment.objective: Observe how seeds contain the blueprint for life, demonstrating God's intelligent design in creation.
procedure: Fold a paper towel and dampen it with water
Place 3-4 bean seeds on the damp towel
Fold the towel over the seeds and place in a clear plastic bag
Seal the bag and place on a sunny windowsill
Observe daily for 7-10 days, keeping the towel moist
experiment.observations: Students will observe roots emerging first (seeking water and nutrients), then the shoot emerging (seeking light). The seed contains all the genetic information needed to produce a complete plant.
experiment.biblical_worldview: Just as God designed seeds with the information to produce 'plants after their kind,' He has designed all living things with purpose and order. The seed demonstrates: 1) Information requires an intelligent source, 2) Life comes from life (biogenesis), 3) Organisms reproduce after their kind (Genesis 1).

water_cycle.title: The Water Cycle - God's Recycling System
water_cycle.scripture_connection: Ecclesiastes 1:7 - All the rivers run into the sea, yet the sea is not full; to the place from which the rivers come, there they return again.

experiment.objective: Model the water cycle and understand God's sustainable design for Earth's water system.
procedure: Fill the large bowl with 2 inches of water
Place the empty cup in the center of the bowl (it should not have water in it)
Cover the bowl tightly with plastic wrap
Place a small rock on the plastic wrap directly above the cup, creating a depression
Put the setup in a sunny location
Observe over 24-48 hours
experiment.observations: Water evaporates from the bowl, condenses on the plastic wrap, and drips into the cup - modeling evaporation, condensation, and precipitation.
experiment.biblical_worldview: The water cycle, described in Ecclesiastes thousands of years before modern science, shows God's wisdom in creating a self-sustaining system. The same water that existed at creation is recycled through this elegant design, providing for all living things generation after generation.

photosynthesis_demo.title: Photosynthesis - Plants as God's Food Factories
photosynthesis_demo.scripture_connection: Genesis 1:29-30 - Then God said, 'Behold, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is on the surface of all the earth, and every tree which has fruit yielding seed; it shall be food for you.'

experiment.objective: Demonstrate that plants produce oxygen through photosynthesis, showing God's provision for all breathing creatures.
procedure: Fill the jar with water (add a pinch of baking soda for extra CO2)
Place the plant in the jar, stems up
Invert the funnel over the plant
Fill a test tube with water and invert it over the funnel stem (no air bubbles)
Place in bright sunlight
Observe bubbles rising from the plant and collecting in the test tube
experiment.observations: Oxygen bubbles will rise from the plant and displace water in the test tube, proving that plants produce oxygen during photosynthesis.
experiment.biblical_worldview: God designed plants to convert sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide into food and oxygen - exactly what animals and humans need to survive. This interdependent design shows intentional creation, not random chance. Plants give us oxygen; we give them carbon dioxide. This is symbiosis by design.

density_layers.title: Density and the Flood - Sorting by Design
density_layers.scripture_connection: Genesis 7:11-12 - On that day all the fountains of the great deep burst forth, and the windows of the heavens were opened. And rain fell upon the earth forty days and forty nights.

experiment.objective: Demonstrate how liquids and solids of different densities naturally separate into layers, illustrating how geological layers could form rapidly during catastrophic events.
materials: Small objects (grape, plastic bead, cork, metal washer)
procedure: Pour honey into the jar (bottom layer)
Slowly pour dish soap on top
Add colored water carefully
Pour vegetable oil
Finally add rubbing alcohol
Drop in small objects and observe where they settle
experiment.observations: Liquids form distinct layers based on density. Objects settle at different levels based on their density relative to the liquids.
experiment.biblical_worldview: This demonstrates that layers can form rapidly when materials of different densities are mixed and allowed to settle - supporting the possibility of rapid geological layer formation during the Genesis Flood, rather than requiring millions of years. God's physical laws (like density) work the same whether processes are slow or catastrophic.

dna_extraction.title: DNA Extraction - The Language of Life
dna_extraction.scripture_connection: Psalm 139:13-14 - For you formed my inward parts; you knitted me together in my mother's womb. I praise you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made.

experiment.objective: Extract DNA from strawberries to visualize the information-carrying molecule that contains the instructions for life.
procedure: Mash 2 strawberries in a ziplock bag
Mix 1 tbsp dish soap, 1 tsp salt, and 1/2 cup water
Add soap mixture to strawberries and gently mix
Filter mixture through coffee filter into glass
Slowly pour cold rubbing alcohol down the side of the glass
Watch DNA precipitate at the alcohol-water interface
experiment.observations: White, stringy DNA will appear at the boundary between the water and alcohol layers. This is the genetic code that contains instructions for making a strawberry.
experiment.biblical_worldview: DNA is a language - a code that requires an intelligent author. The information in DNA doesn't arise from matter and energy alone; information requires intelligence. This points to God as the Author of the genetic code, the One who 'knitted us together' with purpose and design.
